description_en: The action or fact of legally taking another's child and bringing it up as one's own.
example: They started the adoption process after learning they couldn't have biological children.

description_en: One's family or ethnic descent.
example: She traced her ancestry back to a small village in Ireland.

description_en: The relationship between brothers.
example: The soldiers shared a strong sense of brotherhood after surviving the battle.
note: Bond of mutual support and friendship.

description_en: The state of being a child.
example: We had a very happy childhood growing up in the countryside.

description_en: The state or quality of being dedicated to a cause, activity, etc.
example: A long-term relationship requires a high level of commitment from both partners.

meaning: sự kết nối; mối quan hệ
description_en: A relationship in which a person, thing, or idea is linked or associated with something else.
example: The mother and baby shared an instant, deep connection.

description_en: A person, plant, or animal that is descended from a particular ancestor.
example: He is a direct descendant of the famous explorer.

meaning: sự tận tâm; lòng thành kính
description_en: Love, loyalty, or enthusiasm for a person, activity, or cause.
example: Her devotion to her children was evident in everything she did.

meaning: sự ghẻ lạnh; sự ly biệt
description_en: The fact of no longer being on friendly terms or part of a social group.
example: The family went through a long period of estrangement after the argument.

description_en: A family that extends beyond the nuclear family, consisting of parents like aunts, uncles, and cousins.
example: We gathered with our whole extended family for Thanksgiving.
note: Includes relatives beyond parents and siblings.

description_en: Faithfulness to a person, cause, or belief, demonstrated by continuing loyalty and support.
example: Marital fidelity is a core value in many cultures.

description_en: Bring up (a child that is not one's own by birth).
example: They have decided to foster children who are in need of a temporary home.
note: Temporary or transitional care for children.

description_en: All of the people born and living at about the same time, regarded collectively.
example: The younger generation is more comfortable with technology.
note: Group of individuals within a specific age range.

description_en: A child of one's son or daughter.
example: They love spending their weekends playing with their grandchildren.

description_en: Property that is or may be inherited; an inheritance.
example: The family has a rich cultural heritage that they passed down for generations.
note: Traditions and history inherited from ancestors.

description_en: An intense but short-lived passion or admiration for someone or something.
example: His crush on the new girl was just a brief infatuation.

description_en: A thing that is inherited.
example: She used her inheritance to start her own business.
note: Assets or traits passed down after death.

description_en: Close familiarity or friendship; closeness.
example: The couple shared a deep level of emotional intimacy.
note: Sharing of personal thoughts and feelings.

description_en: Blood relationship.
example: Kinship ties remain strong in many rural communities.

description_en: Lineal descent from an ancestor; ancestry or pedigree.
example: She is proud of her royal lineage and family history.

meaning: tư cách làm mẹ; thuộc về sản khoa
description_en: Motherhood.
example: She is currently on maternity leave after giving birth to her son.

meaning: chế độ một vợ một chồng
description_en: The practice or state of being married to one person at a time.
example: Serial monogamy is common in modern Western societies.

description_en: A son of one's brother or sister.
example: My nephew is starting his first year at university this fall.

description_en: A daughter of one's brother or sister.
example: Her niece is a talented young artist and painter.

description_en: A couple and their dependent children, regarded as a basic social unit.
example: The nuclear family remains a dominant social structure.

description_en: Care for and encourage the growth or development of.
example: It's important to nurture talent and creativity in children.
note: Emotional and physical support for growth.

description_en: A person's child or children.
example: Birds defend their offspring with great courage.

description_en: The state of being a father.
example: A paternity test was requested to confirm the biological father.

meaning: phả hệ; dòng dõi thuần chủng
description_en: The record of descent of an animal, showing it to be purebred.
example: The family has a long and distinguished pedigree in the law profession.
note: Record of ancestors (also used for animals).

description_en: A descendant or the descendants of a person, animal, or plant; offspring.
example: They are proud of their talented progeny.

description_en: The restoration of friendly relations.
example: After years of silence, the brothers finally sought reconciliation.

description_en: A person connected by blood or marriage.
example: All our close relatives gathered for the family reunion.

description_en: Each of two or more children or offspring having one or both parents in common; a brother or sister.
example: I have two siblings, one older brother and one younger sister.

description_en: The relationship between sisters.
example: The feminist movement emphasized the importance of global sisterhood.
note: Bond of mutual support and friendship among women.

description_en: A husband or wife, considered in relation to their partner.
example: You are welcome to invite your spouse to the office dinner.
note: Formal term for a marriage partner.

meaning: sự quản lý; sự chăm sóc
description_en: The job of supervising or taking care of something.
example: Good stewardship of family resources is important for future generations.

meaning: người thay thế; người mang thai hộ
description_en: A substitute, especially a person deputizing for another in a specific role or office.
example: The couple used a surrogate mother to have their baby.
note: Taking the place of someone else.

description_en: A young child who is just beginning to walk.
example: The park was full of noisy toddlers and their parents.

description_en: The treatment and instruction received by a child from its parents throughout its childhood.
example: Their strict upbringing had a lasting effect on their behavior.

description_en: A woman who has lost her spouse by death and has not remarried.
example: The elderly widow lived alone in a large house.
note: Female who has lost a husband.

description_en: A man whose spouse has died and who has not remarried.
example: The widower found it hard to adjust to living alone after 50 years of marriage.
note: Male who has lost a wife.

description_en: Give way to arguments, demands, or pressure.
example: Successful families know when to yield and compromise in an argument.
note: Giving in for the sake of harmony.

description_en: A diploid cell resulting from the fusion of two haploid gametes.
example: The development of a human begins with a single zygote.
note: Initial stage of a new organism.

description_en: A relationship between people or groups based on shared feelings, interests, or experiences.
example: The bond between twins is often said to be unbreakable.

description_en: Familiarity or intimacy in a relationship.
example: The brothers share a remarkable closeness despite living in different countries.

description_en: The state of relying on or being controlled by someone or something else.
example: Young children have a high level of dependence on their primary caregivers.

meaning: mối quan hệ; sự đính hôn
description_en: A formal agreement to get married.
example: We celebrated their engagement with a large family party.

description_en: An occasion when many members of an extended family get together travel to see each other.
example: We hold a family reunion at our grandparents' house every five years.

description_en: A person legally entitled to the property or rank of another on that person's death.
example: He is the sole heir to his father's vast estate.

description_en: A house and its occupants regarded as a unit.
example: She manages the daily tasks of a large household.
note: Includes all people living under one roof.
